bead.title: registry: plugin lookup precedence resolver (project → global → baked-in) + topology spec
bead.description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills/personas/plugins can live in two install layers (project-scoped under ddxroot.Path() and machine-wide under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/). Runtime callers need a single authoritative resolver that picks the right on-disk plugin directory with a defined precedence, otherwise the dual-location install surface has no consumer and project installs cannot shadow global ones.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:22 ResolvePlugin must implement precedence project → global → baked-in. The project path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), "plugins", name) (resolve.go:23); the global path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.GlobalDir(), "plugins", name) (resolve.go:28); baked-in (defaultplugin.FS()) is valid only for name == "ddx" (resolve.go:33). ddxroot.GlobalDir() is defined at cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:90.
- Before this layer existed, install/update targeted ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and there was no precedence rule, so an operator with both a project and a global copy had undefined resolution.

PROPOSED FIX
1. ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name) returns (path, layer, err): when both project and global copies exist, return the project path with layer "project"; when only the global copy exists, return its path with layer "global"; when neither exists and name == "ddx", return ("", "baked-in", nil) so callers fall back to defaultplugin.FS(); otherwise return a non-nil error naming all three layers.
2. The project path resolves through ddxroot.Path() so it is correct in BOTH in-tree mode (<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>) and convention mode (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>).
3. SPEC IMPACT: file/update the skill-install-topology ADR (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent) documenting the three-layer precedence, and update the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section to state the project-over-global-over-baked-in lookup precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).
- Does NOT migrate any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Does NOT add agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- Writing the plugin content / creating agent-tier links is handled by the install and init child beads, not here.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project path routes through ddxroot.Path() and the global path through ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on. Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2: skills/personas install-to-agent vs install-in-project).
bead.acceptance:
1. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go:23) asserts that when both the project and global trees contain the plugin, ResolvePlugin returns the project path and layer "project".
2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go:52) asserts that when the project copy is absent, ResolvePlugin returns the ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name> path and layer "global".
3. A test asserts ResolvePlugin returns ("", "baked-in", nil) for name == "ddx" when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name.
4. The skill-install-topology ADR and the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" precedence note are landed in this bead's commit.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
